segments.text: Hear ye now what the LORD saith;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Arise, contend thou before the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hear ye, O mountains, the LORD's controversy, and ye strong foundations of the earth: for the LORD hath a controversy with his people, and he will plead with Israel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: O my people, what have I done unto thee? and wherein have I wearied thee? testify against me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I brought thee up out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed thee out of the house of servants; and I sent before thee Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: O my people, remember now what Balak king of Moab consulted, and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him from Shittim unto Gilgal; that ye may know the righteousness of the LORD.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Wherewith shall I come before the LORD, and bow myself before the high God? shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves of a year old?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Will the LORD be pleased with thousands of rams, or with ten thousands of rivers of oil? shall I give my firstborn for my transgression, the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the LORD require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God?
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: walk humbly: Heb. humble thyself to walk

segments.text: The LORD's voice crieth unto the city, and the man of wisdom shall see thy name: hear ye the rod, and who hath appointed it.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: the man of wisdom: or, thy name shall see that which is

segments.text: Are there yet the treasures of wickedness in the house of the wicked, and the scant measure that is abominable?
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: Is there yet unto every man an house of the, etc
scant measure: Heb. measure of leanness
heading: Judgment on Wickedness

segments.text: Shall I count them pure with the wicked balances, and with the bag of deceitful weights?
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes: count them pure: or, be pure with, etc

segments.text: For the rich men thereof are full of violence, and the inhabitants thereof have spoken lies, and their tongue is deceitful in their mouth.
Therefore also will I make thee sick in smiting thee, in making thee desolate because of thy sins.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thou shalt eat, but not be satisfied; and thy casting down shall be in the midst of thee; and thou shalt take hold, but shalt not deliver; and that which thou deliverest will I give up to the sword.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thou shalt sow, but thou shalt not reap; thou shalt tread the olives, but thou shalt not anoint thee with oil; and sweet wine, but shalt not drink wine.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For the statutes of Omri are kept, and all the works of the house of Ahab, and ye walk in their counsels; that I should make thee a desolation, and the inhabitants thereof an hissing: therefore ye shall bear the reproach of my people.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: For the statutes: or, For he doth much keep the, etc
heading: Judgment on Idolatry
